div.div: The system shall protect batteries against deep discharge.
The system shall use commercially available CubeSat subsystems when feasible.
The system shall implement multiple independent inhibits preventing activation during launch.
The system shall record logs of received and executed commands.
The onboard computer shall be able to switch payload power on and off under ground command and autonomous fault or power-management logic.
The system shall provide a Safe Mode that prioritizes spacecraft survival and basic communications.
The system shall store telemetry and received user messages until they can be downlinked.
The system shall provide configurable transmission parameters.
The system shall operate within a power budget that accounts for a 95 minute orbit with 30–40 minutes of eclipse.
The onboard computer must be able to handle and recover from transient faults such as radiation induced upsets.
Any deployable elements shall remain stowed until after deployment confirmation.
The system shall accept and execute commands from ground operators.
The system shall operate autonomously between ground contacts without continuous operator supervision.
The system shall support remote software updates
The system must authenticate commands before execution.
Subsystem costs should typically fall between €5k and €40k.
The system shall provide sufficient data storage to retain at least 72 hours of expected user traffic and operational logs under typical traffic assumptions.
The system shall support a receiver duty cycle between 10% and 50% of each orbital period, subject to available power margins.
The system shall implement safeguards to keep itself within operational temperature.
The system shall transmit spacecraft health information to ground operators.
The system must relay messages from ground users to radio ground stations.
The mechanical design shall comply with the standard 3U CubeSat form factor (approximately 10 × 10 × 34 cm) with no protrusions during launch.
The system hardware cost shall remain on the order of €200k.
At end-of-life, the system shall disable all transmitters and passivate the battery.
The system shall provide a recovery or rollback mechanism to restore a previous working configuration if an update fails.
The system shall be capable of downlinking stored messages and telemetry during approximately 2–4 ground station passes per day, each with 5–10 minutes of usable link time.
The system shall provide electrical and data interfaces to support operation of onboard payloads.
The system shall delay radio transmission for a minimum of 30 minutes after deployment.
